job.position: Full Stack Engineer - MCP & Micro App Development

strengths: 12+ years of professional engineering experience across frontend-heavy and full-stack roles
Strong TypeScript, JavaScript, React, and Angular record across remote product teams
Clear connector and data-normalization experience across APIs, FTP, SOAP XML feeds, and ETL workflows
Hands-on Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker, and multi-cloud delivery exposure
Hybrid cloud and DevOps collaboration experience across Azure, AWS, and GCP environments
Cross-functional delivery with product, infrastructure, and client-facing collaboration
Relevant AI adjacency through an OpenAI-based proof of concept and practical AI tooling side projects

gaps.item: No direct MCP server or MCP tooling experience is documented
Micro frontend architecture is not explicitly named in the resume, even though there is strong modular frontend and shared-component work
AI orchestration evidence is adjacent rather than deep production ownership
No direct media asset management systems or metadata standards background is documented

resume_focus_priority: TypeScript, React, and Angular frontend ownership
Integration and data-transformation work across complex source systems
Kubernetes, Terraform, cloud-native, and CI/CD delivery evidence
AI-adjacent work that stays grounded in the resume
Cross-functional execution across product, UX, engineering, and infrastructure

evidence.bullet: Senior Full Stack Engineer with 12+ years of professional experience building production web applications, backend integrations, and cloud-native systems across B2B SaaS, enterprise platforms, marketplaces, and Web3 security.

Co-led OpenZeppelin Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a React and TypeScript frontend with Zustand so auditors could track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports with clients.
Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system, cutting build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes in local and CI runs while reducing bundle size and speeding up pull request cycles.

must_have_evidence_map.requirement: Strong proficiency with modern frontend frameworks

evidence.bullet: Co-led OpenZeppelin Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a React and TypeScript frontend with Zustand so auditors could track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports with clients.
Built a React and TypeScript presentation feature in MyPremise that surfaced up-to-date, often live data through a single shareable URL for account managers and clients.
Modernized core parts of the stack by moving from ASP.NET to Angular and from .NET Framework to .NET Core, removing Internet Explorer-only constraints and enabling modern browser support.
Standardized UI work with Material UI and Storybook, reducing prototyping time from one week to one day and making shared components easier to reuse.
Led architecture decisions and set engineering standards for TypeScript, React, and React Native across the company.

must_have_evidence_map.requirement: Experience with MCP servers and tools

evidence.bullet: Built SDK capabilities for Defender and OSS Relayer so technical teams could integrate the products and build custom automation tooling.
Led development of an internal integration and onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, removing dependency on external engineering support and saving $75,000 annually.

must_have_evidence_map.requirement: Hands-on Kubernetes, Terraform, and cloud-native architectures

evidence.bullet: Tech stack includes Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS, and Docker in a production product environment.
Introduced DevOps practices and led a staged migration from on-prem systems to Azure hybrid infrastructure across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.
Cloud & DevOps section includes Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker, AWS, GCP, and Azure.

must_have_evidence_map.requirement: Familiarity with AI orchestration, schema mediation, and LLM-based workflows

evidence.bullet: Built an internal hackathon proof of concept with the OpenAI API for natural-language report generation.

Built backend integrations for merchant ingestion pipelines, normalizing data from FTP, APIs, SOAP XML feeds, and web scraping to support pricing, stock, availability, bundles, and product attributes.
Developed ETL pipelines that ingested and normalized product data from manual uploads, APIs, web scraping, and FTP CSV files, supporting catalogs with more than 5,000 products.

must_have_evidence_map.requirement: Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills

evidence.bullet: Worked directly with clients through discovery calls, demos, and status updates, translating requirements into shipped products under short deadlines.
Improved architecture and delivery for a legacy internal platform, working across product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to reduce delivery cycles from months to about two weeks.
